schema.description: A comprehensive support ticket management system that handles user authentication, ticket lifecycle operations, commenting, and analytics for customer support workflows.

tools.name: login
tools.description: Authenticate a user with credentials and establish an active session for accessing ticket system operations.

username.description: The username credential for authenticating the user account

password.description: The password credential for authenticating the user account

success.description: Indicates whether the authentication attempt was successful

session_token.description: The session token issued upon successful authentication, used for subsequent authenticated requests

username.description: The username of the successfully authenticated user account

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Terminate the current user session and invalidate the authentication token.

success.description: Indicates whether the logout operation completed successfully

tools.name: check_login_status
tools.description: Verify the current authentication state and retrieve active session information.

is_authenticated.description: Indicates whether a valid user session is currently active

username.description: The username of the currently authenticated user account, if a session is active

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Create a new support ticket with specified details, priority, and optional assignment. Requires an authenticated session.

title.description: The title or subject line summarizing the ticket issue or request

description.description: Detailed description of the issue, request, or context for the ticket. Defaults to empty string if not provided.

priority.description: Priority level of the ticket ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest priority). Defaults to 1 if not specified.

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report. Defaults to 'general' if not provided.

assignee.description: The username of the staff member to assign this ticket to. If not provided, the ticket remains unassigned.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created ticket

title.description: The title or subject line of the created ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket, which is 'open' for newly created tickets

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the authenticated user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if an assignee was specified

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive details about a specific ticket using its unique identifier.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to retrieve
The unique identifier of the ticket

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed.

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

updated_at.description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the ticket, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

resolution.description: Detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided, if the ticket has been resolved

tools.name: list_tickets
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tickets with optional filtering by status, priority, category, or assignee. Requires an authenticated session.

status.description: Filter tickets by their current status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, returns tickets of all statuses.

priority.description: Filter tickets by priority level, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest). If not provided, returns tickets of all priority levels.

category.description: Filter tickets by category. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report. If not provided, returns tickets of all categories.

assignee.description: Filter tickets by the username of the assigned staff member. If not provided, returns tickets for all assignees including unassigned tickets.

limit.description: Maximum number of tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

offset.description: Number of tickets to skip before returning results, used for pagination. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of ticket summaries matching the specified filters

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

tools.name: search_tickets
tools.description: Search for tickets by matching keywords in their title or description fields. Requires an authenticated session.

query.description: The search keyword or phrase to match against ticket titles and descriptions

status.description: Filter search results by ticket status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, searches across all statuses.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of tickets matching the search query

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: update_ticket
tools.description: Modify one or more fields of an existing ticket with partial update support. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to update

title.description: New title or subject line for the ticket

description.description: New detailed description for the ticket

priority.description: New priority level for the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: New category for the ticket. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report.

assignee.description: New assignee username for the ticket. Provide an empty string to remove the current assignee.

status.description: New status for the ticket. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated ticket

status.description: Confirmation message indicating the update operation was successful

updated_fields.description: List of field names that were modified during the update operation

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was updated, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: close_ticket
tools.description: Mark a ticket as closed without providing resolution details. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to close
The unique identifier of the closed ticket

status.description: The updated status of the ticket, which is 'closed'

message.description: Confirmation message indicating the ticket was successfully closed

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was closed, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: resolve_ticket
tools.description: Mark a ticket as resolved and record the resolution details explaining how the issue was addressed. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to resolve

resolution.description: Detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided to address the issue

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the resolved ticket

status.description: The updated status of the ticket, which is 'resolved'

resolution.description: The detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided

resolved_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was marked as resolved, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was updated, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_user_tickets
tools.description: Retrieve all tickets created by a specific user or the currently authenticated user. Requires an authenticated session.

username.description: The username to filter tickets by. If not provided, returns tickets created by the currently authenticated user.

status.description: Filter tickets by their current status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, returns tickets of all statuses.

limit.description: Maximum number of tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of tickets created by the specified user

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a ticket, with support for public or internal (staff-only) visibility. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to add a comment to

comment.description: The text content of the comment to add to the ticket

is_internal.description: If true, the comment is marked as internal and visible only to staff members. If false, the comment is public. Defaults to false.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created comment

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket the comment was added to

comment.description: The text content of the comment

author.description: The username of the authenticated user who authored the comment

is_internal.description: Indicates whether the comment is internal (staff-only) or public

created_at.description: The timestamp when the comment was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_ticket_comments
tools.description: Retrieve all comments associated with a specific ticket, with optional inclusion of internal staff-only comments. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to retrieve comments for

include_internal.description: If true, includes internal staff-only comments in the results (requires staff privileges). If false, returns only public comments. Defaults to false.

comments.description: A list of comments associated with the ticket

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket the comment belongs to

comment.description: The text content of the comment

author.description: The username of the user who authored the comment

is_internal.description: Indicates whether the comment is internal (staff-only) or public

created_at.description: The timestamp when the comment was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_ticket_statistics
tools.description: Retrieve aggregated statistics and metrics about tickets in the system for a specified time period. Requires an authenticated session.

time_period.description: The time period to calculate statistics for. Valid values are: today, week, month, year, or all. Defaults to 'all' if not specified.

total_tickets.description: The total count of tickets in the system for the specified time period

open_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'open' status

in_progress_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'in_progress' status

resolved_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'resolved' status

closed_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'closed' status

average_resolution_time.description: The average time in hours taken to resolve tickets during the specified time period

tickets_by_category.description: A breakdown of ticket counts grouped by category (technical, billing, general, feature_request, bug_report)

tickets_by_priority.description: A breakdown of ticket counts grouped by priority level (1 through 5)

scenarios.description: Basic ticket system with one regular user, one staff member, and a single open ticket

1.title: Cannot login to account
1.description: I'm having trouble logging into my account. Password reset doesn't work.

scenarios.description: Active support system with multiple users, tickets in various states, and comments

2.title: Feature request: Dark mode
2.description: Would love to have a dark mode option for the interface

1.comment: I've escalated this to the payment team. Investigating now.
1.author: support1

2.comment: Thank you for reporting this. We've identified the issue.
2.author: manager

scenarios.description: Comprehensive ticket system with diverse users, multiple ticket categories, priorities, extensive comment threads, and various ticket states

1.title: Critical: Database connection timeout
1.description: Production database is timing out. Multiple users affected. Error: Connection timeout after 30s.

2.title: Refund request for order #12345
2.description: Product arrived damaged. Requesting full refund.
2.resolution: Refund processed. Amount credited to original payment method.

3.title: How to export data?
3.description: I need to export my data to CSV format. Can't find the option in settings.

4.title: API rate limit too restrictive
4.description: Current rate limit of 100 req/min is insufficient for our use case. Need at least 500 req/min.

5.title: Mobile app crashes on startup
5.description: iOS app version 3.2.1 crashes immediately after launch on iPhone 12.

6.title: Account verification email not received
6.description: Signed up 2 hours ago but haven't received verification email. Checked spam folder.
6.resolution: Email was blocked by spam filter. Manually verified account and whitelisted domain.

7.title: Documentation outdated
7.description: API documentation shows deprecated endpoints. Please update to reflect v3 API.

1.comment: Investigating database logs. Appears to be connection pool exhaustion.
1.author: admin

2.comment: We're aware of the issue and working on a fix. ETA 2 hours.
2.author: admin

3.comment: Can you provide photos of the damaged product?
3.author: support1

5.comment: Escalating to product team for review.
5.author: manager

6.comment: Can you provide crash logs from your device?
6.author: support2

7.comment: Crash logs indicate memory leak in image loading module.
7.author: support2

scenarios.description: Edge cases: empty system, extreme priority values, midnight timestamps, empty descriptions, and very long text fields

2.title: Maximum priority urgent issue with extremely long title that describes a very complex problem involving multiple systems and requiring immediate attention from senior staff members
2.description: This is a very detailed description that contains a lot of information about the issue. It includes technical details, error messages, stack traces, and reproduction steps. The description goes on and on to test the system's ability to handle large text fields. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.
2.resolution: Resolved after extensive investigation and coordination with multiple teams. Root cause identified and permanent fix deployed.

1.comment: This is an internal note with a very long comment that tests the system's ability to handle large amounts of text in comments. It contains detailed technical analysis, debugging steps, and coordination notes between team members. The comment continues with more information about the investigation process and findings.
1.author: support3

errors.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
errors.error_details: After successful login (which returns a session_token), subsequent tool calls fail with 'Authentication required' error. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after login succeeds.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Each tool call appears to have no authentication context, despite login returning success=true and a valid session_token.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass empty strings ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...)). The login tool returns a session_token but there's no mechanism to store or pass this token to subsequent tool calls. The system likely expects the session_token to be passed with each authenticated request, but the MCP tool layer doesn't implement this session token management.

errors.error_location: MCP tool wrapper functions - session token parameter handling
errors.error_details: All authenticated tools call their underlying system methods with hardcoded empty string ('') for the session_token parameter. For example: system.list_tickets('', status, priority, ...), system.create_ticket('', title, description, ...), etc.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tools should either maintain session state internally or accept session_token as a parameter. Actual: Tools hardcode empty session_token, making authentication impossible.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool layer doesn't implement proper session management. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class methods expect a session_token parameter, but the MCP @mcp.tool() wrappers don't expose this parameter to callers and instead pass empty strings. This creates a disconnect between the authentication mechanism (login returns a token) and the authorization mechanism (tools need the token but don't accept or use it).

errors.error_location: All authenticated tools (list_tickets, search_tickets, create_ticket, update_ticket, close_ticket, resolve_ticket, get_user_tickets, add_comment, get_ticket_comments, get_ticket_statistics)
errors.error_details: Tools requiring authentication consistently fail with 'Authentication required' error despite successful login operations. The login tool returns success and generates session tokens, but subsequent tool calls do not recognize the authenticated session.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: After successful login, authenticated tools should execute successfully using the established session. Actual: All authenticated tools fail with 'Authentication required' error, indicating the session is not being maintained or passed correctly between tool invocations.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass an empty string ('') as the session_token parameter to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.list_tickets('', ...), system.search_tickets('', ...)). The tools do not retrieve or pass the session token established by the login operation. This architectural issue means that while login creates a session, no mechanism exists to pass that session token to subsequent tool calls, making all authenticated operations fail.

errors.error_details: The tool wrapper functions are designed to accept parameters but do not include session_token as a parameter. They hardcode empty strings when calling the underlying system methods. For example: system.list_tickets('', status, priority, ...) instead of system.list_tickets(session_token, status, priority, ...).
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool wrappers should either accept session_token as a parameter or retrieve it from a shared session context. Actual: Tool wrappers hardcode empty string for session_token, making authentication impossible.
errors.root_cause: Fundamental design flaw in the MCP tool layer. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class expects session tokens to be passed to authenticated methods, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a mechanism to pass these tokens. This creates a disconnect between the authentication system (which works) and the authorization system (which cannot verify sessions).

errors.error_location: Authentication system - affects all authenticated tools
errors.error_details: The login tool successfully creates a session token (e.g., 'session_customer1_1767258632'), but this session is not persisted or recognized by subsequent tool calls. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after a successful login, and all other tools requiring authentication fail with 'Authentication required' errors.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: After successful login, check_login_status should return is_authenticated=true and username should be set. All authenticated tools should work. Actual: check_login_status returns is_authenticated=false, username='', and all authenticated tools fail with authentication errors.
errors.root_cause: The session management implementation does not properly maintain session state across tool invocations. The login tool wrapper passes an empty string ('') as the session_token parameter to system methods (e.g., system.login(username, password)), but the actual session token is not being stored or retrieved in a way that persists across MCP tool calls. The logout tool also passes an empty string: system.logout(''), and check_login_status passes system.check_login_status(''). This suggests the tool wrappers are not designed to handle stateful sessions across multiple tool invocations in the MCP context.

errors.error_location: All MCP tool wrappers requiring authentication
errors.error_details: The tool wrappers consistently pass empty strings or omit session_token parameters when calling the underlying system methods. For example: system.create_ticket('', title, ...), system.list_tickets('', status, ...), etc. This indicates the MCP tools are not designed to accept or pass session tokens from the client.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tools should either accept session_token as a parameter or maintain session state internally. Actual: Tools pass empty strings for session tokens and have no mechanism to maintain authentication state.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool layer does not implement a session management strategy. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class likely expects session tokens to be passed with each method call, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a way for clients to pass these tokens or maintain them internally between calls.

errors.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
errors.error_details: After calling login() successfully and receiving a session token, subsequent calls to check_login_status() return is_authenticated=false. All authenticated operations fail with 'Authentication required' error despite successful login.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Session state is not maintained between tool calls, requiring re-authentication for each operation.
errors.root_cause: The tool wrapper functions pass empty string ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.list_tickets('', ...), etc.). The session token returned from login is not being captured and passed to subsequent tool calls. This is a critical design flaw in the MCP tool wrappers - they don't maintain session state between calls.

errors.error_details: All tool wrappers (except login) pass empty string or hardcoded empty session_token to the underlying system methods. For example: system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...). This prevents any authenticated operations from working.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool wrappers should either maintain session state internally or accept session_token as a parameter. Actual: Tool wrappers hardcode empty session tokens, making authentication system non-functional.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool layer doesn't implement session management. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class expects session tokens to be passed to each method, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a mechanism to pass or store these tokens.

sample_error.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
sample_error.error_details: After successful login (which returns a session_token), subsequent tool calls fail with 'Authentication required' error. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after login succeeds.
sample_error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Each tool call appears to have no authentication context, despite login returning success=true and a valid session_token.
sample_error.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass empty strings ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...)). The login tool returns a session_token but there's no mechanism to store or pass this token to subsequent tool calls. The system likely expects the session_token to be passed with each authenticated request, but the MCP tool layer doesn't implement this session token management.

sample_error.error_details: The tool wrapper functions are designed to accept parameters but do not include session_token as a parameter. They hardcode empty strings when calling the underlying system methods. For example: system.list_tickets('', status, priority, ...) instead of system.list_tickets(session_token, status, priority, ...).

sample_error.root_cause: Fundamental design flaw in the MCP tool layer. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class expects session tokens to be passed to authenticated methods, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a mechanism to pass these tokens. This creates a disconnect between the authentication system (which works) and the authorization system (which cannot verify sessions).

errors.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
errors.error_details: After successful login (which returns a session_token), subsequent tool calls fail with 'Authentication required' error. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after login succeeds.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Each tool call appears to have no authentication context, despite login returning success=true and a valid session_token.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass empty strings ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...)). The login tool returns a session_token but there's no mechanism to store or pass this token to subsequent tool calls. errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: After successful login, authenticated tools should execute successfully using the established session. Actual: All authenticated tools fail with 'Authentication required' error, indicating the session is not being maintained or passed correctly between tool invocations.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass an empty string ('') as the session_token parameter to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.list_tickets('', ...), system.search_tickets('', ...)). The tools do not retrieve or pass the session token established by the login operation. This architectural issue means that while login creates a session, no mechanism exists to pass that session token to subsequent tool calls, making all authenticated operations fail.

errors.error_details: The tool wrapper functions are designed to accept parameters but do not include session_token as a parameter. They hardcode empty strings when calling the underlying system methods. For example: system.list_tickets('', status, priority, ...) instead of system.list_tickets(session_token, status, priority, ...).
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool wrappers should either accept session_token as a parameter or retrieve it from a shared session context. Actual: Tool wrappers hardcode empty string for session_token, making authentication impossible.
errors.root_cause: Fundamental design flaw in the MCP tool layer. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class expects session tokens to be passed to authenticated methods, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a mechanism to pass these tokens. This creates a disconnect between the authentication system (which works) and the authorization system (which cannot verify sessions).

errors.error_location: Authentication system - affects all authenticated tools
errors.error_details: The login tool successfully creates a session token (e.g., 'session_customer1_1767258632'), but this session is not persisted or recognized by subsequent tool calls. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after a successful login, and all other tools requiring authentication fail with 'Authentication required' errors.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: After successful login, check_login_status should return is_authenticated=true and username should be set. All authenticated tools should work. Actual: check_login_status returns is_authenticated=false, username='', and all authenticated tools fail with authentication errors.
errors.root_cause: The session management implementation does not properly maintain session state across tool invocations. The login tool wrapper passes an empty string ('') as the session_token parameter to system methods (e.g., system.login(username, password)), but the actual session token is not being stored or retrieved in a way that persists across MCP tool calls. The logout tool also passes an empty string: system.logout(''), and check_login_status passes system.check_login_status(''). This suggests the tool wrappers are not designed to handle stateful sessions across multiple tool invocations in the MCP context.

errors.error_location: All MCP tool wrappers requiring authentication
errors.error_details: The tool wrappers consistently pass empty strings or omit session_token parameters when calling the underlying system methods. For example: system.create_ticket('', title, ...), system.list_tickets('', status, ...), etc. This indicates the MCP tools are not designed to accept or pass session tokens from the client.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tools should either accept session_token as a parameter or maintain session state internally. Actual: Tools pass empty strings for session tokens and have no mechanism to maintain authentication state.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool layer does not implement a session management strategy. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class likely expects session tokens to be passed with each method call, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a way for clients to pass these tokens or maintain them internally between calls.

errors.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
errors.error_details: After calling login() successfully and receiving a session token, subsequent calls to check_login_status() return is_authenticated=false. All authenticated operations fail with 'Authentication required' error despite successful login.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Session state is not maintained between tool calls, requiring re-authentication for each operation.
errors.root_cause: The tool wrapper functions pass empty string ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.list_tickets('', ...), etc.). The session token returned from login is not being captured and passed to subsequent tool calls. This is a critical design flaw in the MCP tool wrappers - they don't maintain session state between calls.

errors.error_details: All tool wrappers (except login) pass empty string or hardcoded empty session_token to the underlying system methods. For example: system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...). This prevents any authenticated operations from working.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tool wrappers should either maintain session state internally or accept session_token as a parameter. Actual: Tool wrappers hardcode empty session tokens, making authentication system non-functional.
errors.root_cause: The MCP tool layer doesn't implement session management. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class expects session tokens to be passed to each method, but the MCP tool wrappers don't provide a mechanism to pass or store these tokens.

error.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
error.error_details: After successful login (which returns a session_token), subsequent tool calls fail with 'Authentication required' error. The check_login_status tool returns is_authenticated=false immediately after login succeeds.
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Each tool call appears to have no authentication context, despite login returning success=true and a valid session_token.
error.root_cause: The MCP tool wrappers pass empty strings ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.check_login_status(''), system.list_tickets('', ...)). The login tool returns a session_token but there's no mechanism to store or pass this token to subsequent tool calls. The system likely expects the session_token to be passed with each authenticated request, but the MCP tool layer doesn't implement this session token management.

error.error_location: Authentication system - session persistence across tool calls
error.error_details: After calling login() successfully and receiving a session token, subsequent calls to check_login_status() return is_authenticated=false. All authenticated operations fail with 'Authentication required' error despite successful login.
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Session should persist across tool calls within the same client_id. Actual: Session state is not maintained between tool calls, requiring re-authentication for each operation.
error.root_cause: The tool wrapper functions pass empty string ('') as session_token to the underlying system methods (e.g., system.logout(''), system.list_tickets('', ...), etc.). The session token returned from login is not being captured and passed to subsequent tool calls. This is a critical design flaw in the MCP tool wrappers - they don't maintain session state between calls.

error.error_location: MCP tool wrapper functions - session token parameter handling
error.error_details: All authenticated tools call their underlying system methods with hardcoded empty string ('') for the session_token parameter. For example: system.list_tickets('', status, priority, ...), system.create_ticket('', title, description, ...), etc.
error.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Tools should either maintain session state internally or accept session_token as a parameter. Actual: Tools hardcode empty session_token, making authentication impossible.
error.root_cause: The MCP tool layer doesn't implement proper session management. The underlying TicketManagementSystem class methods expect a session_token parameter, but the MCP @mcp.tool() wrappers don't expose this parameter to callers and instead pass empty strings. This creates a disconnect between the authentication mechanism (login returns a token) and the authorization mechanism (tools need the token but don't accept or use it).
